primary_focus: Reusable component design: ActionButton with useTransition + action prop, generic ToggleGroup, reusable ConfirmDialog. TypeScript generics for reusable components.

action_button_signals.signal: ActionButton component with action prop and internal useTransition
action_button_signals.trigger: Requirements 5, 8, 17, 20: pending button behavior as a single reusable component
action_button_signals.correct: A reusable button component that accepts an `action` (or `clickAction`) prop typed as `() => Promise<void>` instead of the usual `onClick`. Internally, the component wraps the action in `useTransition` (via `startTransition(() => action())`) or uses the action as a form action. It reads `isPending` from the transition to show the pending label and disable the button. Each button instance has its own independent transition state — clicking 'Start' on task A does not affect 'Delete' on task B.
action_button_signals.why_action_prop: React 19's documentation recommends that components accepting async callbacks use a prop named 'action' (or similar) to signal that the callback will be wrapped in a transition/action. This convention communicates the async contract to consumers — they know the callback can be async and the component handles the pending state automatically.
action_button_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Each action button manages its own isPending useState + try/catch. The pending logic is duplicated across every button usage. This works but violates DRY and the spec requirement for a single reusable component.
action_button_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: A reusable button with onClick + manual isPending state (useState + setState(true) before the call, setState(false) after). This is reusable but misses useTransition — the pending state is handled imperatively instead of declaratively. useTransition also provides better integration with React's concurrent features (the pending update is a transition, so it doesn't block urgent updates).
action_button_signals.anti_pattern_tier3: A reusable button with onClick that accepts a sync callback. The consumer wraps the async logic and passes isPending as a separate prop. The component is not self-contained — the pending state management leaks to every call site.

toggle_group_signals.trigger: Requirements 11-12, 21: same toggle pattern for both status and priority filters
toggle_group_signals.correct: A single reusable ToggleGroup<T> component with generic type parameter. Props: options: { value: T; label: string }[], value: T, onChange: (value: T) => void. The component renders a group of buttons and manages the active state visually. Used twice — once for status filter, once for priority filter — with different option types.
toggle_group_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Two separate filter components (StatusFilter, PriorityFilter) with duplicated toggle logic. Violates the spec requirement for a single reusable component.
toggle_group_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: A reusable component but typed with string instead of a generic — loses type safety at the call site (onChange receives string instead of TaskStatus | 'all').

confirm_dialog_signals.trigger: Requirements 15-17, 22: confirmation dialog reusable and not task-specific
confirm_dialog_signals.correct: A generic ConfirmDialog component with props: open, title, message, confirmLabel, onConfirm (async action), onCancel. Uses the <dialog> element for native modal behavior. The confirm button is an ActionButton (reusing the same pending button pattern). Closing via Escape or backdrop click calls onCancel.
confirm_dialog_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: Delete confirmation logic hardcoded inline in the task component. Not reusable.
confirm_dialog_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: A reusable dialog but implemented as a div overlay without <dialog>. Requires manual focus trapping and Escape handling.

form_submission_signals.signal: Form submission via native form or action pattern
form_submission_signals.trigger: Requirements 8-10: create task form with pending button and Enter-to-submit
form_submission_signals.correct: The creation form uses a <form> element. The ActionButton component works as a submit button within the form. Enter in the title input naturally submits the form. The action (creating a task) is async and the button shows pending state during the API call.
form_submission_signals.anti_pattern_tier1: onClick on the button + onKeyDown on the input checking for Enter. Reimplements native form behavior.
form_submission_signals.anti_pattern_tier2: <form onSubmit> with e.preventDefault() + manual isPending state. Works but doesn't leverage the action button pattern.

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: filteredTasks
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirements 11-14: tasks filtered by status and priority
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Computed via useMemo from tasks array, statusFilter, and priorityFilter
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.anti_pattern: Stored as useState and synced via useEffect

derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.name: emptyStateMessage
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.trigger: Requirements 18-19: different messages for no matches vs no tasks
derived_values_that_must_not_be_state.correct: Derived from tasks.length and filteredTasks.length during render

legitimate_effect_uses.expected_pattern: useEffect with empty dependency array calling fetchTasks(), or Suspense-based approach

typescript_signals.correct: ToggleGroup<T extends string> with typed options and onChange callback. Call sites get type-safe values: ToggleGroup<TaskStatus | 'all'>
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: ToggleGroup with options: { value: string; label: string }[] — loses type safety

typescript_signals.signal: ActionButton action prop typed as async function
typescript_signals.correct: action: () => Promise<void> or action: () => void | Promise<void> — clearly communicates async intent
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: onClick: () => void — consumers must handle isPending themselves

typescript_signals.correct: onConfirm: () => Promise<void> — aligns with ActionButton's action prop pattern
typescript_signals.anti_pattern: onConfirm: () => void with separate isPending prop

accessibility_signals.correct: Button has aria-busy={isPending} during pending state. Label change ('Starting...' etc.) is sufficient for screen readers if the button text updates.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No indication of pending state for assistive technology

accessibility_signals.correct: <dialog> element with showModal(). Browser handles focus trapping, Escape key, and backdrop. aria-labelledby points to the dialog title.
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: Div overlay with manual focus trap implementation

accessibility_signals.correct: role='group' with aria-label, each button has aria-pressed={isSelected}
accessibility_signals.anti_pattern: No ARIA — visually styled buttons with no programmatic indication of selection

accessibility_signals.correct: Error notification has role='alert' or aria-live='assertive'

expected_components: ActionButton (reusable pending button with action prop)
TaskItem (individual task row with action buttons)
